scenario: Vertex Insurer ships a Camunda 8.8 DMN model with twelve decisions that compute claim eligibility for 4000 requests per day. The architect must identify the XML element that represents the DRG itself in the file, since a new audit tool flags every DRG by its root element name at deployment time across the DMN repository.
question: Which XML element represents the Decision Requirements Graph in a Camunda 8.8 DMN file?

options.text: The drg element wraps every decision in the DMN file and is the root used by the Zeebe DMN evaluator at deployment time
The definitions element wraps every decision in the DMN file and is the root used by the Zeebe DMN evaluator at deployment time
The diagram element wraps every decision in the DMN file and is the root used by the Zeebe DMN evaluator at deployment time
The decisionGraph element wraps every decision in the DMN file and is the root used by the Zeebe DMN evaluator at deployment time

a.text: Incorrect. The DMN schema has no drg root element; the docs state that in XML a Decision Requirements Graph is represented by the definitions element with the DMN namespace.

b.text: Correct. The DMN docs state that in the XML a DRG is represented by the definitions element, which carries the DMN namespace and contains the decision elements of the graph.

c.text: Incorrect. The diagram element belongs to the diagram interchange layout layer of DMN, not the DRG root; the DRG itself is the definitions element of the file.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no decisionGraph element in the DMN schema; the DRG is represented by the definitions root element that contains the decision elements.

explanation: A Decision Requirements Graph models the dependencies between decisions, input data, and knowledge sources; in the XML the DRG itself is the definitions root element that contains the individual decision elements of the model.
